author: @puremadmentalmackay
text: Ahhh the kaleidoscope, invited by Mr Brewster from my home town of Jedburgh! There is a block in a wall near the main pedestrian bridge across the river Jed commemorating him and his invention.

Nie vždy chcem, a môžem  pozerať vaše video cez PC, kde to ide.

author: @sojournerdelaterra6144
text: That was so cool. Inspired to want to build one myself, LOL. Oh, I noticed even the nice little nod to the psychedelic 1960's as well with the music when you showed the kaleidoscope in action. ;-)

author: @ladymondegreen312
text: Some years ago I went on a tour of the Kremlin in Moscow. In there they have a strongroom where they display some of the precious possessions of the Romanov family as well as showcasing the vast wealth of Russia itself. There are huge gemstones, massive gold nuggets and exquisite jewellery but the only thing I really coveted was a kaleidoscope that had belonged to one of the Romanov children. It was made of gold and instead of having coloured glass to make the pretty patterns it had real gemstones.
